title: Personal Autonomy as an Ethical Foundation for Opaque Algorithmic Decision Systems
description_es: Este artículo examina la autonomía personal como base ética fundamental para sistemas de decisión algorítmica opacos, proponiendo principios para preservar la agencia humana en contextos de IA.
abstract: As algorithmic decision systems become increasingly opaque, preserving personal autonomy becomes a critical ethical concern. This paper argues that personal autonomy should serve as the foundational ethical principle for designing and deploying opaque algorithmic systems. We propose a framework for maintaining human agency and meaningful choice in AI-mediated decision contexts.

citations.apa: Lara, F. (2026). Personal Autonomy as an Ethical Foundation for Opaque Algorithmic Decision Systems. AI Ethics, 6, 14.
citations.mla: Lara, Francisco. "Personal Autonomy as an Ethical Foundation for Opaque Algorithmic Decision Systems." AI Ethics, vol. 6, 2026, p. 14.
